Long-term care hospital (LTCH) NA - Patient was not discharged from an inpatient facility (M1005) Inpatient Discharge Date (Most Recent): 04/17/2026 UK Unknown Primary Reason for Home Health/Medical Necessity (POC Element): Patient is a 48-year-old female with a history of HTN, asthma, seizure disorder, chronic pain syndrome with polyneuropathy and spinal cord stimulator, and anxiety/depression, who was admitted to inpatient rehabilitation on 04/06/2026 following an acute event in late March/early April. On 03/30/2026 she was last seen well at ~1600 and found unresponsive at ~1800; EMS transported her to the hospital where she required naloxone (Narcan) for marked somnolence. Initial workup demonstrated Hgb 10.5, Na 145 and a urine toxicology screen positive for benzodiazepines. Her hospital course was notable for persistent altered mental status with episodic abnormal movements (described as twitching/rigid movements and repetitive words), an aspiration event with transient hypoxia, and need for bilateral wrist restraints and lower-extremity splints for safety. CT head and CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed no acute intracranial or cardiopulmonary process; EEG did not demonstrate seizure activity. She also sustained a recent fall with right lower-extremity injury and has been immobilized in a cast (now transitioned to CAM boot). At inpatient rehab she is sitting up in bed, cooperative with exam, reports intermittent high pain scores though appears comfortable during evaluation. Current management includes gabapentin and PRN analgesics; she reports inconsistent home use of baclofen and agrees to resume at a lower dose given concerns for withdrawal and potential seizure-threshold effects. She has no fever, chills, chest pain or shortness of breath. Functionally she has significant decline from baseline independence - requiring supervision/assistance for bed mobility, transfers and ambulation and will require PT/OT (ROM, gait/transfer training, ADL retraining) and nursing/wound care for ongoing right knee/ankle wounds noted on exam.
